---
jupyter:
  jupytext:
    formats: ipynb,Rmd
    text_representation:
      extension: .Rmd
      format_name: rmarkdown
      format_version: '1.2'
      jupytext_version: 1.14.5
  kernelspec:
    display_name: Python 3 (ipykernel)
    language: python
    name: python3
---

```{python colab={'base_uri': 'https://localhost:8080/', 'height': 17}, executionInfo={'elapsed': 343, 'status': 'ok', 'timestamp': 1680164102047, 'user': {'displayName': 'Tom Laclavère', 'userId': '17855265856500373237'}, 'user_tz': -120}, id="e83ba096", outputId="cfe75d6a-9daa-44dc-f99e-2bd3027e2609"}
from IPython.display import display, HTML
display(HTML("<style>.container { width:95% !important; }</style>"))
```

```{python colab={'base_uri': 'https://localhost:8080/', 'height': 373}, executionInfo={'elapsed': 20, 'status': 'error', 'timestamp': 1680163843473, 'user': {'displayName': 'Tom Laclavère', 'userId': '17855265856500373237'}, 'user_tz': -120}, id="23ea68da", outputId="5c6ef756-fc39-4ef8-9bd4-4158c2137373"}
# QUBIC packages
import qubic
from qubicpack.utilities import Qubic_DataDir
import sys

# Put the path where acquisition.py is
sys.path.append('/home/tom/mypackages')

import frequency_acquisition as Acq


# Display packages
import healpy as hp
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import gc

# General packages
import numpy as np
from importlib import reload
from pyoperators import *

def integration(nu_min, nu_max, Nintegr, sky_config, d, fwhm_subbands = None):
    print(f'Integration from {nu_min:.2f} to {nu_max:.2f} GHz with {Nintegr} steps')
    obj = Acq.QubicIntegrated(d, Nsub=Nintegr, Nrec=Nintegr)
    if Nintegr == 1:
        allnus = np.array([np.mean([nu_min, nu_max])])
    else:
        allnus = np.linspace(nu_min, nu_max, Nintegr)
    m = obj.get_PySM_maps(sky_config, nus=allnus)
    
    if fwhm_subbands is not None:
        for i in range(Nintegr):
            C = HealpixConvolutionGaussianOperator(fwhm=fwhm_subbands[i])
            m[i] = C(m[i])
    
    return np.array([np.mean(m, axis=0)])


# PyOperators packages
from pyoperators import (
    BlockColumnOperator, BlockDiagonalOperator, BlockRowOperator,
    CompositionOperator, DiagonalOperator, I, IdentityOperator,
    MPIDistributionIdentityOperator, MPI, proxy_group, ReshapeOperator,
    rule_manager, pcg, Operator)

from pysimulators.interfaces.healpy import HealpixConvolutionGaussianOperator
```

```{python id="b4b28bf5", outputId="7a12ea69-c405-4ce3-c0fe-729e1b9e468d"}
reload(Acq)
nside = 256
relative_bandwidth = 0.25
band = 220
seed = 42
noiseless = False
effective_duration = 3
tol = 1e-40
noise_qubic_level = 1


npointings = 3000
Nf_TOD = 1
Nf_recon = 1
fact_sub = 1
```

```{python id="d47b7ee8"}
dictfilename = 'dicts/pipeline_demo.dict'

# Read dictionary chosen
d_TOD = qubic.qubicdict.qubicDict()
d_TOD.read_from_file(dictfilename)

d_TOD['nf_recon'] = Nf_TOD
d_TOD['nf_sub'] = Nf_TOD
d_TOD['nside'] = nside
npix=12*d_TOD['nside']**2
d_TOD['RA_center'] = 0
d_TOD['DEC_center'] = -57
center = qubic.equ2gal(d_TOD['RA_center'], d_TOD['DEC_center'])
d_TOD['effective_duration'] = effective_duration
d_TOD['npointings'] = npointings
d_TOD['tol'] = 5e-4
d_TOD['filter_nu'] = band * 1e9
d_TOD['photon_noise'] = False#not noiseless
d_TOD['noiseless'] = noiseless
d_TOD['config'] = 'FI'
d_TOD['MultiBand'] = True
d_TOD['planck'] = True
d_TOD['dtheta'] = 15
```

```{python id="09650a3d", outputId="12ac6395-f741-4846-88d7-a026a3a21b45"}
qubic_acquisition = Acq.QubicIntegrated(d_TOD, Nsub=Nf_TOD, Nrec=Nf_TOD)

qubic_acquisition
```

```{python id="33d60acc", outputId="65c72813-4f56-4002-8d3c-1048c606894e"}
H = qubic_acquisition.get_operator()

H
```

```{python id="5fec635c", outputId="75a0aadf-98ef-4875-b52d-7f10396fbffd"}
invN = qubic_acquisition.get_invntt_operator()

invN
```

```{python id="1e75298e"}
Nf_TOD = 1
Nf_recon = 1
fact_sub = 1

sky_config = {'cmb':1}#, 'dust':'d0'}
```

```{python id="5daf5efb", outputId="d71360ac-6122-4275-efb5-41455725412c"}
# Create Nsub maps
m_sub = qubic_acquisition.get_PySM_maps(sky_config, qubic_acquisition.allnus)
qubic_acquisition = Acq.QubicIntegrated(d_TOD, Nsub=Nf_TOD, Nrec=Nf_TOD)

# Create operator
H_TOD = qubic_acquisition.get_operator(convolution=False, myfwhm=None)
n_qubic = qubic_acquisition.get_noise()

# Create coverage
coverage = qubic_acquisition.get_coverage()
seenpix = coverage/coverage.max() > 0.0001

# Create TOD
TOD_QUBIC = H_TOD(m_sub[0]).ravel() + (n_qubic.ravel() * noise_qubic_level)
```

```{python id="e963f871", outputId="212f82f0-e0e3-4a58-9b2e-cbfc7ddd785e"}
H = qubic_acquisition.get_operator()
invN = qubic_acquisition.get_invntt_operator()

R = ReshapeOperator(H.shapeout, H.shape[0])

H = R * H
```

```{python id="a5a9fdec"}
A = H.T * invN * H
b = H.T * invN * TOD_QUBIC.ravel()
```

```{python id="451242be", outputId="4a5859bc-4212-4d43-f735-5559cb209a9f"}
solution = pcg(A, b, x0=m_sub[0], disp=True, tol=1e-5, maxiter=300)
```

```{python id="9f9268f5", outputId="cef0df14-4a8e-4079-cc0f-1535d2795365"}
# %matplotlib inline
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 10))

m_sub[:, ~seenpix, :] = hp.UNSEEN
solution['x'][~seenpix, :] = hp.UNSEEN
stk = ['I', 'Q', 'U']

k=0
reso=25
for i in range(3):
    if i == 0:
        m = 300
        minr = 20
    else:
        m = 8
        minr = 8
        
    hp.gnomview(m_sub[0, :, i], rot=center, reso=reso, cmap='jet', sub=(3, 3, k+1), min=-m, max=m, title=f'{stk[i]} - Input')
    hp.gnomview(solution['x'][:, i], rot=center, reso=reso, cmap='jet', sub=(3, 3, k+2), min=-m, max=m, title=f'{stk[i]} - Output')
    residuals = solution['x'][:, i]-m_sub[0, :, i]
    residuals[~seenpix] = hp.UNSEEN
    hp.gnomview(residuals, rot=center, reso=reso, cmap='jet', sub=(3, 3, k+3), min=-minr, max=minr, title=f'{stk[i]} - Residuals')
    k+=3

plt.show()
```

<!-- #region id="0f652356" -->
You should see above the reconstruction of QUBIC maps for I, Q and U for monochromatic acquisition. The reconstruction is quite good in the center of the patch and shows the sensitivity of QUBIC to CMB polarization. An undesirable effect that is widely visible is the poor reconstruction of the edges due to the multiple peaks of the synthetic beam. By moving to the edges of the coverage, some peaks are positioned outside and therefore these pixels are very badly reconstructed. This effect is transmitted from one to the other until an acceptable reconstruction is obtained in the center of the patch.
<!-- #endregion -->

<!-- #region id="f90f9e4c" -->
## Monochromatic acquisition with Planck data <a class="anchor" id="section_3_2"></a>
<!-- #endregion -->

```{python id="f2d06309", outputId="944d4329-b383-44d2-8f86-3c1a60e34d48"}
del H
gc.collect()
```

```{python id="94c0c194", outputId="b09e350e-efee-4924-abb6-54f718f995d5"}
# Planck acquisition
planck_acquisition = Acq.PlanckAcquisition(217, qubic_acquisition.scene)

# Joint acquisition
qubicplanck_acquisition = Acq.QubicPlanckMultiBandAcquisition(qubic_acquisition, planck_acquisition)

H_qp = qubicplanck_acquisition.get_operator()
```

```{python id="6c78d896"}
invN = qubicplanck_acquisition.get_invntt_operator()
```

```{python id="6ddfb2ce", outputId="a425c65e-4705-4c72-9822-79e9d7b84747"}
band_planck = 217
bw = 35
Nintegr = 10

planck_obs = integration(band_planck-bw/2, band_planck+bw/2, Nintegr, sky_config, d_TOD, fwhm_subbands = None)
planck_noise = planck_acquisition.get_noise()

TOD_PLANCK = planck_obs[0] + planck_noise

TOD = np.r_[TOD_QUBIC.ravel(), TOD_PLANCK.ravel()]
```

```{python id="bcc5301c"}
A = H_qp.T * invN * H_qp
b = H_qp.T * invN * TOD
```

```{python id="1cd5f463", outputId="055844cf-040e-4b6d-dc8e-3b41ff721471"}
solution_qp = pcg(A, b, x0=None, disp=True, tol=1e-5, maxiter=300)
```

```{python id="4777a4ac", outputId="c1aa5d5e-5070-4dfb-a2ee-86f938f1de33"}
# %matplotlib inline
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 10))

#m_sub[:, ~seenpix, :] = hp.UNSEEN
#solution['x'][~seenpix, :] = hp.UNSEEN
stk = ['I', 'Q', 'U']

k=0
reso=25
for i in range(3):
    if i == 0:
        m = 300
        minr = 20
    else:
        m = 8
        minr = 8
        
    hp.gnomview(m_sub[0, :, i], rot=center, reso=reso, cmap='jet', sub=(3, 3, k+1), min=-m, max=m, title=f'{stk[i]} - Input')
    hp.gnomview(solution_qp['x'][:, i], rot=center, reso=reso, cmap='jet', sub=(3, 3, k+2), min=-m, max=m, title=f'{stk[i]} - Output')
    residuals = solution_qp['x'][:, i]-m_sub[0, :, i]
    residuals[~seenpix] = hp.UNSEEN
    hp.gnomview(residuals, rot=center, reso=reso, cmap='jet', sub=(3, 3, k+3), min=-minr, max=minr, title=f'{stk[i]} - Residuals')
    k+=3

plt.show()
```

```{python id="7a20679e"}
del H_qp
gc.collect()
```

<!-- #region id="e1f84653" -->
## Broadband acquisition <a class="anchor" id="section_3_3"></a>

An important thing to keep in mind is that here we have simulated the reconstruction of a monochromatic instrument (which is not real). We will now see how to simulate a broadband instrument.
<!-- #endregion -->

<!-- #region id="37e34a2f" -->
<img src="make_tod.png" alt="" style="width:1000px;height:420px;">

$$\text{TOD} = H_{\nu_0} \cdot m_{\nu_0} + H_{\nu_1} \cdot m_{\nu_1} + \cdots + H_{\nu_N} \cdot m_{\nu_N}$$
$$\text{TOD} = \sum_{i=0}^{N_{sub}} H_{\nu_i} m_i$$
<!-- #endregion -->

```{python id="2aa7f480"}
Nf_TOD = 4
Nf_recon = 1
fact_sub = 4

sky_config = {'cmb':1}#, 'dust':'d0'}
```

<!-- #region id="5c9a1bc4" -->
We now redefine those acquisitions with broadband integration.
<!-- #endregion -->

```{python id="7452e6da", outputId="9b90f66a-4aa8-4d0e-f51d-1872084c9f41"}
# Create Nsub maps
qubic_acquisition = Acq.QubicIntegrated(d_TOD, Nsub=Nf_TOD, Nrec=Nf_TOD)
m_sub = qubic_acquisition.get_PySM_maps(sky_config, qubic_acquisition.allnus)


# Create operator
H_TOD = qubic_acquisition.get_operator(convolution=False, myfwhm=None, fixed_data=None)
n_qubic = qubic_acquisition.get_noise()

# Create coverage
coverage = qubic_acquisition.get_coverage()
seenpix = coverage/coverage.max() > 0.0001

# Create TOD
TOD_QUBIC = H_TOD(m_sub).ravel() + (n_qubic.ravel() * noise_qubic_level)

TOD = np.r_[TOD_QUBIC.ravel(), TOD_PLANCK.ravel()]
```

<!-- #region id="321dfe9d" -->
Let's define again the joint acquistion with Planck.
<!-- #endregion -->

```{python id="330bc5ec", outputId="faa95f11-75f0-4092-9cc5-a22bc21ae62e"}
# QUBIC acquisition
qubic_acquisition = Acq.QubicIntegrated(d_TOD, Nsub=Nf_recon*fact_sub, Nrec=Nf_recon)

# Planck acquisition
planck_acquisition = Acq.PlanckAcquisition(217, qubic_acquisition.scene)

# Joint acquisition
qubicplanck_acquisition = Acq.QubicPlanckMultiBandAcquisition(qubic_acquisition, planck_acquisition)

H_qp = qubicplanck_acquisition.get_operator()
```

```{python id="3b234c9c"}
invN = qubicplanck_acquisition.get_invntt_operator()
```

```{python id="3eb58c90", outputId="9a9436a4-0cc0-47c9-f369-29a383ef4025"}
H_qp
```

```{python id="8c87a759"}
A = H_qp.T * invN * H_qp
b = H_qp.T * invN * TOD
```

```{python id="f5493c4b", outputId="d69ca9e0-392d-41af-c27a-f9a1fc499923"}
solution_qp_broadband = pcg(A, b, x0=None, disp=True, tol=1e-5, maxiter=300)
```

```{python id="2febc33f", outputId="f8ba8be7-70b5-476f-f6f8-8be08001b210"}
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 10))

#m_sub[:, ~seenpix, :] = hp.UNSEEN
#solution['x'][~seenpix, :] = hp.UNSEEN
stk = ['I', 'Q', 'U']

k=0
reso=25
for i in range(3):
    if i == 0:
        m = 300
        minr = 20
    else:
        m = 8
        minr = 8
        
    hp.gnomview(m_sub[0, :, i], rot=center, reso=reso, cmap='jet', sub=(3, 3, k+1), min=-m, max=m, title=f'{stk[i]} - Input')
    hp.gnomview(solution_qp_broadband['x'][:, i], rot=center, reso=reso, cmap='jet', sub=(3, 3, k+2), min=-m, max=m, title=f'{stk[i]} - Output')
    residuals = solution_qp_broadband['x'][:, i]-m_sub[0, :, i]
    residuals[~seenpix] = hp.UNSEEN
    hp.gnomview(residuals, rot=center, reso=reso, cmap='jet', sub=(3, 3, k+3), min=-minr, max=minr, title=f'{stk[i]} - Residuals')
    k+=3

plt.show()
```

<!-- #region id="4105ec23" -->
We should see that we don't have difference between this result and with the previous one. We assumed in simulations pure CMB with no astrophysical emission. In the real case, we should have broadband acquisition with multiple emission especially CMB + Thermal Dust which leads to have several effects. Let's simulate them.
<!-- #endregion -->

<!-- #region id="c618c608" -->
## Broadband acquisition with astrophysical emissions <a class="anchor" id="section_3_4"></a>

Let's assume now that we have not only CMB inside our data but also atrophysical emission like thermal dust or synchrotron emission.
<!-- #endregion -->

```{python id="b29d8d23", outputId="68fe5b9b-22a9-4a3e-a67f-1b2c3aa5892c"}
Nf_TOD = 4
Nf_recon = 1
fact_sub = 4

sky_config = {'cmb':1, 'dust':'d0'}       # We modify our model

# Create Nsub maps
qubic_acquisition = Acq.QubicIntegrated(d_TOD, Nsub=Nf_TOD, Nrec=Nf_TOD)
m_sub = qubic_acquisition.get_PySM_maps(sky_config, qubic_acquisition.allnus)


# Create operator
H_TOD = qubic_acquisition.get_operator(convolution=False, myfwhm=None, fixed_data=None)
n_qubic = qubic_acquisition.get_noise()

# Create coverage
coverage = qubic_acquisition.get_coverage()
seenpix = coverage/coverage.max() > 0.0001

planck_obs = integration(band_planck-bw/2, band_planck+bw/2, Nintegr, sky_config, d_TOD, fwhm_subbands = None)
planck_noise = planck_acquisition.get_noise()

TOD_PLANCK = planck_obs[0] + planck_noise

# Create TOD
TOD_QUBIC = H_TOD(m_sub).ravel() + (n_qubic.ravel() * noise_qubic_level)

TOD = np.r_[TOD_QUBIC.ravel(), TOD_PLANCK.ravel()]
```

```{python id="94ed2e43", outputId="cb118263-23cf-4065-ab9e-43a461065ba7"}
# QUBIC acquisition
qubic_acquisition = Acq.QubicIntegrated(d_TOD, Nsub=Nf_recon*fact_sub, Nrec=Nf_recon)

# Planck acquisition
planck_acquisition = Acq.PlanckAcquisition(217, qubic_acquisition.scene)

# Joint acquisition
qubicplanck_acquisition = Acq.QubicPlanckMultiBandAcquisition(qubic_acquisition, planck_acquisition)

H_qp = qubicplanck_acquisition.get_operator()
invN = qubicplanck_acquisition.get_invntt_operator()
```

<!-- #region id="629c8f48" -->
Let's compute again the A and b term.
<!-- #endregion -->

```{python id="1ba2ca24"}
A = H_qp.T * invN * H_qp
b = H_qp.T * invN * TOD
```

<!-- #region id="26f6b321" -->
Let's find the optimal solution.
<!-- #endregion -->

```{python id="627eb475", outputId="d81b0bdc-139f-4c6b-b3b4-2bd8cadda428"}
solution_qp_broadband_with_dust = pcg(A, b, x0=None, disp=True, tol=1e-5, maxiter=100)
```

```{python id="54a9e169"}
mysolution = solution_qp_broadband_with_dust['x'].copy()
mysolution[~seenpix] = planck_obs[0][~seenpix].copy()
```

```{python id="ec76bb89", outputId="3ffca9a2-434e-40e2-e750-08fc3ddfb460"}
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 10))

#m_sub[:, ~seenpix, :] = hp.UNSEEN
mysolution[~seenpix, :] = hp.UNSEEN
stk = ['I', 'Q', 'U']

k=0
reso=25
for i in range(3):
    if i == 0:
        m = 300
        minr = 20
    else:
        m = 8
        minr = 8
        
    hp.gnomview(np.mean(m_sub[:, :, i], axis=0), rot=center, reso=reso, cmap='jet', sub=(3, 3, k+1), min=-m, max=m, title=f'{stk[i]} - Input')
    hp.gnomview(mysolution[:, i], rot=center, reso=reso, cmap='jet', sub=(3, 3, k+2), min=-m, max=m, title=f'{stk[i]} - Output')
    residuals = mysolution[:, i]-np.mean(m_sub[:, :, i], axis=0)
    residuals[~seenpix] = hp.UNSEEN
    hp.gnomview(residuals, rot=center, reso=reso, cmap='jet', sub=(3, 3, k+3), min=-minr, max=minr, title=f'{stk[i]} - Residuals')
    k+=3

plt.show()
```

<!-- #region id="8d11e9fe" -->
## Inclusion of convolution <a class="anchor" id="section_3_5"></a>
<!-- #endregion -->

<!-- #region id="bb9f4f4a" -->
In the previous code, we assume no convolution which leads to have reconstruction of data with infinite angular resolution. We have in reality to take into account convolutions during the process. 

<img src="convolution.png" alt="" style="width:1200px;height:500px;">

To do that, we include convolution operator which reconvolve each sub-acquisitions to the expected angular resolution. In order to do that, we need to reconstruct the final frequency map at **the best angular resolution**. 
<!-- #endregion -->

```{python id="b202d7c6", outputId="5af77cf9-0c7d-42f4-b59e-1be1dba4842c"}
Nf_TOD = 2
Nf_recon = 1
fact_sub = 2

# Create Nsub maps
qubic_acquisition = Acq.QubicIntegrated(d_TOD, Nsub=Nf_TOD, Nrec=Nf_TOD)
m_sub = qubic_acquisition.get_PySM_maps(sky_config, qubic_acquisition.allnus)


# Create operator
H_TOD = qubic_acquisition.get_operator(convolution=True)
n_qubic = qubic_acquisition.get_noise()

# Create coverage
coverage = qubic_acquisition.get_coverage()
seenpix = coverage/coverage.max() > 0

# Create TOD
TOD_QUBIC = H_TOD(m_sub).ravel() + (n_qubic.ravel() * noise_qubic_level)


############################
####### Planck data ########
############################

band_planck = 217
bw = 35
Nintegr = 10

planck_obs = integration(band_planck-bw/2, band_planck+bw/2, Nintegr, sky_config, d_TOD, fwhm_subbands = None)
planck_noise = planck_acquisition.get_noise()
C = HealpixConvolutionGaussianOperator(fwhm=np.min(qubic_acquisition.allfwhm))
TOD_PLANCK = C(planck_obs[0] + planck_noise)

TOD = np.r_[TOD_QUBIC.ravel(), TOD_PLANCK.ravel()]
```

```{python id="671fe64c", outputId="bc9feb91-92f2-4e86-ea34-605a47a2c611"}
myfwhm = np.sqrt(qubic_acquisition.allfwhm**2 - np.min(qubic_acquisition.allfwhm)**2)
myfwhm
```

```{python id="88854ae8", outputId="aef06b61-d699-4560-9fca-7de169c89586"}
# QUBIC acquisition
qubic_acquisition = Acq.QubicIntegrated(d_TOD, Nsub=Nf_recon*fact_sub, Nrec=Nf_recon)

# Planck acquisition
planck_acquisition = Acq.PlanckAcquisition(217, qubic_acquisition.scene)

# Joint acquisition
qubicplanck_acquisition = Acq.QubicPlanckMultiBandAcquisition(qubic_acquisition, planck_acquisition)

Hconv = qubicplanck_acquisition.get_operator(convolution=True, myfwhm=myfwhm)
```

```{python id="4baafdf5"}
A = Hconv.T * invN * Hconv
b = Hconv.T * invN * TOD
```

```{python id="58a08426", outputId="f960c603-209d-4b13-be42-f6a1c46f4035"}
solution_conv = pcg(A, b, x0=None, disp=True, tol=1e-5, maxiter=100)
```

```{python id="19f9a713"}
mysolution = solution_conv['x'].copy()
mysolution[~seenpix] = planck_obs[0][~seenpix].copy()
```

```{python id="03c3ef78", outputId="22609228-77e6-47d0-b41b-24700c128e14"}
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 10))

#m_sub[:, ~seenpix, :] = hp.UNSEEN
mysolution[~seenpix, :] = hp.UNSEEN
stk = ['I', 'Q', 'U']

k=0
reso=25
for i in range(3):
    if i == 0:
        m = 300
        minr = 20
    else:
        m = 8
        minr = 8
        
    hp.gnomview(C(np.mean(m_sub[:, :, i], axis=0)), rot=center, reso=reso, cmap='jet', sub=(3, 3, k+1), min=-m, max=m, title=f'{stk[i]} - Input')
    hp.gnomview(mysolution[:, i], rot=center, reso=reso, cmap='jet', sub=(3, 3, k+2), min=-m, max=m, title=f'{stk[i]} - Output')
    residuals = mysolution[:, i]-C(np.mean(m_sub[:, :, i], axis=0))
    residuals[~seenpix] = hp.UNSEEN
    hp.gnomview(residuals, rot=center, reso=reso, cmap='jet', sub=(3, 3, k+3), min=-minr, max=minr, title=f'{stk[i]} - Residuals')
    k+=3

plt.show()
```

<!-- #region id="9fa47640" -->
You should see that now we are reconvering cosmological signal convolve by the instrumental beam. This step is very important because it produce realistic simulations. This step is slowing down the code, for each step of the PCG, we need to compute `Nsub - 1` convolution which is very heavy computationally..
<!-- #endregion -->

<!-- #region id="d82627cf" -->
## Multifrequency reconstruction <a class="anchor" id="section_3_6"></a>

The last part is dedicated to the reconstruction of several frequencies within the physical band of QUBIC. Indeed, the beam of QUBIC varies a lot with the frequency which is an essential point for this part and for the component map-making. The frequency information of the captured emissions is in our TOD and must be used.
<!-- #endregion -->

```{python id="9e8e9d60", outputId="ac9032a6-044a-46ee-b80b-64dfb788d725"}
Nf_TOD = 4
Nf_recon = 2
fact_sub = 2

sky_config = {'cmb':1, 'dust':'d0'}       # We modify our model

# Create Nsub maps
qubic_acquisition = Acq.QubicIntegrated(d_TOD, Nsub=Nf_TOD, Nrec=Nf_TOD)
m_sub = qubic_acquisition.get_PySM_maps(sky_config, qubic_acquisition.allnus)


# Create operator
H_TOD = qubic_acquisition.get_operator(convolution=False, myfwhm=None, fixed_data=None)
n_qubic = qubic_acquisition.get_noise()

# Create coverage
coverage = qubic_acquisition.get_coverage()
seenpix = coverage/coverage.max() > 0.0001

planck_obs = integration(band_planck-bw/2, band_planck+bw/2, Nintegr, sky_config, d_TOD, fwhm_subbands = None)
planck_noise = planck_acquisition.get_noise()

TOD_PLANCK = planck_obs[0] + planck_noise

# Create TOD
TOD_QUBIC = H_TOD(m_sub).ravel() + n_qubic.ravel()

TOD = np.r_[TOD_QUBIC.ravel(), TOD_PLANCK.ravel(), TOD_PLANCK.ravel()]
```

```{python id="2463447b", outputId="9b3ef49f-f5c4-4cc5-8e1a-5720e2c26c12"}
# QUBIC acquisition
qubic_acquisition = Acq.QubicIntegrated(d_TOD, Nsub=Nf_recon*fact_sub, Nrec=Nf_recon)

# Planck acquisition
planck_acquisition = Acq.PlanckAcquisition(band_planck, qubic_acquisition.scene)

# Joint acquisition
qubicplanck_acquisition = Acq.QubicPlanckMultiBandAcquisition(qubic_acquisition, planck_acquisition)

H_multifrequency = qubicplanck_acquisition.get_operator()
invN = qubicplanck_acquisition.get_invntt_operator()
```

```{python id="d226060d"}

```

```{python id="2bdc1352"}
A = H_multifrequency.T * invN * H_multifrequency
b = H_multifrequency.T * invN * TOD
```

```{python id="57de7251", outputId="05470552-48fd-43e0-eab1-81aa95b2cb07"}
solution_multifrequency = pcg(A, b, x0=None, disp=True, tol=1e-5, maxiter=100)
```

```{python id="8c577940"}
# Let's play with the cut

seenpix = coverage/coverage.max() > 0.2
mysolution = solution_multifrequency['x'].copy()
mysolution[:, ~seenpix] = planck_obs[0][~seenpix].copy() + planck_noise[~seenpix]

myinputs = np.mean(m_sub[:fact_sub, :, :], axis=0)
myinputs[~seenpix] = planck_obs[0, ~seenpix].copy()
```

```{python id="c6f4f3e6", outputId="7e21097f-de1e-4739-ff87-6eaaa7df6420"}
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 10))

#m_sub[:, ~seenpix, :] = hp.UNSEEN
#mysolution[:, ~seenpix, :] = hp.UNSEEN
stk = ['I', 'Q', 'U']

k=0
reso=25
for i in range(3):
    if i == 0:
        m = 300
        minr = 20
    else:
        m = 8
        minr = 8
        
    hp.gnomview(myinputs[:, i], rot=center, reso=reso, cmap='jet', sub=(3, 3, k+1), min=-m, max=m, title=f'{stk[i]} - Input')
    hp.gnomview(mysolution[0, :, i], rot=center, reso=reso, cmap='jet', sub=(3, 3, k+2), min=-m, max=m, title=f'{stk[i]} - Output')
    residuals = mysolution[0, :, i]-myinputs[:, i]
    #residuals[~seenpix] = hp.UNSEEN
    hp.gnomview(residuals, rot=center, reso=reso, cmap='jet', sub=(3, 3, k+3), min=-minr, max=minr, title=f'{stk[i]} - Residuals')
    k+=3

plt.show()
```

```{python id="619e6790", outputId="096656bb-cd2b-4112-ea04-3669e5b17e61"}
myinputs = np.mean(m_sub[fact_sub:2*fact_sub, :, :], axis=0)
myinputs[~seenpix] = planck_obs[0, ~seenpix].copy()

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 10))

#m_sub[:, ~seenpix, :] = hp.UNSEEN
#mysolution[:, ~seenpix, :] = hp.UNSEEN
stk = ['I', 'Q', 'U']

k=0
reso=25
for i in range(3):
    if i == 0:
        m = 300
        minr = 20
    else:
        m = 8
        minr = 8
        
    hp.gnomview(myinputs[:, i], rot=center, reso=reso, cmap='jet', sub=(3, 3, k+1), min=-m, max=m, title=f'{stk[i]} - Input')
    hp.gnomview(mysolution[1, :, i], rot=center, reso=reso, cmap='jet', sub=(3, 3, k+2), min=-m, max=m, title=f'{stk[i]} - Output')
    residuals = mysolution[1, :, i]-myinputs[:, i]
    #residuals[~seenpix] = hp.UNSEEN
    hp.gnomview(residuals, rot=center, reso=reso, cmap='jet', sub=(3, 3, k+3), min=-minr, max=minr, title=f'{stk[i]} - Residuals')
    k+=3

plt.show()
```

<!-- #region id="2cf49a1a" -->
Here, the specificity is that we use Planck data to regularize the edges of the QUBIC acquisition and it seems to work well. Due to the presence of the dust which is a modified black body and the fact that our model intrinsically assumes a constant emission within a reconstructed band, we have dust residues. These residues are incorrigible and are therefore part of the analysis.

<img src="coverage.png" alt="" style="width:900px;height:400px;">

Moreover, from the above diagram, we know that the Planck acquisition is essentially the same over the whole sky while the QUBIC acquisition is stronger in the center of the patch. In the end, we end up with three distinct areas: 

+ Outside the QUBIC patch, we have the Planck data.
+ Inside the QUBIC patch in the center, we are largely dominated by the sensitivity of QUBIC.
+ Between the two previous regions, the two acquisitions have an equivalent relative weight.

In this last area, since we have a frequency-dependent emission, we can decide to fix these pixels to Planck data since QUBIC is not sensitive enough to see with enough precision (put 0.2 or above).
<!-- #endregion -->

<!-- #region id="19a2cfa4" -->
## General conclusions <a class="anchor" id="chapter4"></a>
<!-- #endregion -->

<!-- #region id="a55815d9" -->
At the end, the frequency map-making is a very important step for the data reprensentation and for the further studies. We always have difficulties that needs to be taking into account like :

+ We considered Nsub sub-acquisitions for the TOD making and the same number for the reconstruction but in reality Nsub tend to infinity for the real TOD.
+ We have astrophysical emission which depend of the frequency and produces bais on the reconstruction because we assume constant emission within a reconstructed sub-band.
<!-- #endregion -->

```{python id="59b9702b"}

```
